title: The starting scene of Episode 25 whats the use of it
display_title: The starting scene of Episode 25 whats the use of it
wikitext:
[]: The starting scene of Episode 25'- what's the use of it?             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Fri May 11, 2007 4:50 am It's been some time since I last opened a topic- it feels good. Okay, I'm not sure whether an exactly same topic has already existed, but from an old thread 'How might Shinji learn to love himself', I was prompted to think a question: Why did Anno add that Room 303 scene to the movie? Shinji is a coward indeed, I'm fully aware of it, but why would the director need to add such an obscene scene?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 11, 2007 7:44 am Think about everything Shinji has been through up to that point. Rei has died and been revealed to be a clone. Asuka has discintegrated to the point where she's been heavily sedated for her own safety. Ritsuko has been jailed. Kaji is dead. Touji's lost his leg. Shinji's friends homes have all been destroyed. Gendo has completely alienated Shinji. Kaworu has been killed at Shinji's hands. Shinji's having a pretty bad month. I can't think of anything more appropriate that could happen at that precise moment to just make him feel even worse. Can you? You may think it's obscene, but I think it's an important and integral part of the film.

[]:             Quote Post by Ejinathan » Fri May 11, 2007 7:50 am I guess there's also were those people of Gainax want to show they knowledge about life. Even tought it continues in ep 26. It's dificult to make a scene to get things explained

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Fri May 11, 2007 8:51 am I don't think masturbation is a good way to express your sadness. Right Shinji has had a bad month, but I still don't see why he should do that to Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 11, 2007 9:11 am tinmeigut wrote: I don't think masturbation is a good way to express your sadness. Right Shinji has had a bad month, but I still don't see why he should do that to Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri May 11, 2007 9:15 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Think about everything Shinji has been through up to that point. Rei has died and been revealed to be a clone. Asuka has discintegrated to the point where she's been heavily sedated for her own safety. Ritsuko has been jailed. Kaji is dead. Touji's lost his leg. Shinji's friends homes have all been destroyed. Gendo has completely alienated Shinji. Kaworu has been killed at Shinji's hands. Shinji's having a pretty bad month. I can't think of anything more appropriate that could happen at that precise moment to just make him feel even worse. Can you? You may think it's obscene, but I think it's an important and integral part of the film.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Fri May 11, 2007 9:44 am Hey Eva Monkey how do you know I'm younger than you? And how much sense does it really make? Please explain. Also, how is strangulation similar to masturbation?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaminoyouni » Fri May 11, 2007 9:48 am tinmeigut wrote: how is strangulation similar to masturbation?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 11, 2007 10:21 am tinmeigut wrote: Hey Eva Monkey how do you know I'm younger than you?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 11, 2007 11:26 am tinmeigut wrote: Also, how is strangulation similar to masturbation?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri May 11, 2007 11:58 am All those horrible things that have happened to Shinji that DatDude and Evamonkey mentioned, Shinji's not going to be in the best mental state. That's the point of that scene, to show how rock-bottom he's hit. Note how even after he finished masturbating, his words, "I'm the lowest of the low" implies IMHO that he feels EVEN WORSE THAN BEFORE. And though Rei gives Shinji the power to decide what to do with "his world", the fact that he ends up choosing to live while knowing Instrumentality is wrong, DESPITE EVERYTHING---ALL THE PAIN, SUFFERING, AND BETRAYAL (inmo, only his preciption of things, as both EoTV and EoE point out: nobody "hates" him, HE wants to think that) HE'S BEEN THROUGH, that to me is beautiful, but that's a topic for another day. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 11, 2007 1:47 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Note how even after he finished masturbating, his words, "I'm the lowest of the low" implies IMHO that he feels EVEN WORSE THAN BEFORE.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri May 11, 2007 2:21 pm The first few minutes of Air get to the basic themes -- sex and death. And Shinji manages to fumble both of them. I'm sure someone could get all po-mo on that.

[]:             Quote Post by Ejinathan » Fri May 11, 2007 2:27 pm Um... i more had the feeling he just couldn't hold his physical love for Asuka and that's why after the disgusting scene he says that he's the lowest. (didn't you people saw he was looking at her ... )Hormones are so dificult to handle at that age too... i wonder why u were thinking about strangulation. (even tough it's possible that i misunderstood you're word here ... )

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri May 11, 2007 2:43 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The first few minutes of Air get to the basic themes -- sex and death. And Shinji manages to fumble both of them. I'm sure someone could get all po-mo on that.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri May 11, 2007 2:48 pm post modern

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri May 11, 2007 2:55 pm @Ejinathan Some English language slang/euphemism for male masturbation likens it to choking (=strangling). As has been said before, EoE starts with Shinji choking his chicken and ending by choking his Asuka. DatDude wrote: po-mo ?

[]:             Quote Post by Ejinathan » Fri May 11, 2007 3:04 pm Mr. Tines wrote: @Ejinathan Some English language slang/euphemism for male masturbation likens it to choking (=strangling). As has been said before, EoE starts with Shinji choking his chicken and ending by choking his Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Fri May 11, 2007 5:49 pm i personally dont think that the scene added anything to the movie besides "WTFrick?!"

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri May 11, 2007 5:56 pm InvaderRen wrote: i personally dont think that the scene added anything to the movie besides "WTFrick?!"
